title: Viva la Vida
source: freemidi.org (full-length transcription, 140 measures; replaced the 552-byte 4-measure bitmidi uploads/24946 fragment, 2026-07-09 data audit)

musicalLanguage.description: Coldplay's 2008 single, winner of the 2009 Grammy for Song of the Year, present here as a full-length 140-measure transcription in 4/4 at 140 BPM — 6,218 notes spanning a range from C1 in the left hand up to C6 in the right. The harness confirms the stated Ab major strongly (statedKeyFit 0.861; the best-fit detected key, G# major, is the identical pitch center spelled with sharps, and the chord labels below keep that sharp spelling). Of the measures the confidence gate labels (61% of the total), the top three labels — Fm at 22%, G# at 14%, and C# at 9% — plus three D#7 windows are, read in flats, the minor vi, the tonic I, the IV, and the dominant V of Ab major: the same four functions as the song's signature rotating loop. Underneath it all, a 36-note left-hand ostinato cell drives nearly half the accompaniment, repeating verbatim seven times across measures 4-79.
musicalLanguage.structure: The section lens divides the 140 measures into 12 units, with its sharpest novelty breaks at measure 96 (score 4.24, the strongest in the song), measure 127 (3.8), measure 103 (3.43), and measure 56 (2.39); the closing unit, measures 127-140, is a quiet outro holding the file's only silent measure (128) and its sparsest playing (measures 129, 130, 132, and 133 carry just 2 onsets each). Large verbatim repeats confirm a cyclic verse-chorus design rather than through-composition: the left hand of measures 35-57 returns note-for-note at measures 75-97 (a 95-note span covering 34% of all left-hand content), the right hand of measures 40-52 returns at measures 79-91, and the unit at measures 103-112 is built from one 4-measure phrase stated at measures 105-108 and repeated exactly at 109-112 in both hands.
keyMoments: Measures 4-11 state the 36-note left-hand ostinato cell that anchors the whole arrangement — it recurs verbatim seven times (measures 4-11, 12-19, 24-31, 32-39, 56-63, 64-71, 72-79), covering 45% of the left hand at a 5.86:1 compression ratio, a textbook example of a pop epic built from one loop.

Measure 96 carries the strongest structural break the section lens finds anywhere in the piece (novelty 4.24), marking the turn out of the verse-chorus cycles into the contrasting unit at measures 103-112, whose repeated 4-measure phrase (105-108 restated exactly at 109-112 in both hands) is a model for practicing repetition that still moves forward.

Both hands reach their peak in the same bar — C6 in the right hand and A#3 in the left at measure 125 — so the arrangement saves its highest point for the end of the last full section before the outro, then empties completely at measure 128, the only silent measure in the file.

The densest playing lands at measures 64 and 79 (13 onsets each, 8 in the right hand over 5 in the left), both inside the ostinato's later spans — a clear example of intensity built through onset density while the Fm / G# / C# harmony keeps rotating underneath.
teachingGoals: Practice the 36-note left-hand ostinato of measures 4-11 until it runs on autopilot — with seven verbatim repetitions covering 45% of the left hand, this single cell is nearly half the song's accompaniment.

Recognize the loop behind the labels: hear Fm as the minor vi and the G# windows as the tonic of the confirmed Ab major (the harness spells it G# major), so the pull back to the tonic stays audible through every rotation of the progression.

Learn the large-scale recycling before drilling bar by bar: the left hand of measures 35-57 returns exactly at measures 75-97, so one well-practiced span pays off twice — and divide the remaining work along the section lens's 12 detected units rather than arbitrary 8-bar chunks.

Build dynamic pacing from the density map: measures 64 and 79 are the 13-onset peaks, measure 128 is silent, and measures 129-133 sit near 2 onsets each, so the outro should feel like the room emptying.
styleTips: Keep the left-hand ostinato metronomically even at the unchanging 140 BPM tempo — the right-hand melody phrases over it, and any push or drag in the loop unseats the whole groove.
Play each label change as an arrival, voicing the C# major and G# major windows with a clear downbeat so the IV-to-tonic motion of the loop lands rather than blurs.

Save the fullest sound for measure 125, where the right hand's C6 and the left hand's A#3 peak together — then cut immediately to nothing for the silent measure 128 and keep measures 129-133 near-solo soft.

Feel measure 96's break (novelty 4.24, the strongest in the song) as a genuine reset: take a breath there, then shape the twin 4-measure phrases of measures 105-112 as call and answer rather than copy and paste.
